articles.title: Doctors on Strike: What Patients Need to Know
articles.summary: In England, some doctors called resident doctors are going on strike for six days starting Tuesday. The NHS, which runs hospitals and clinics, says patients should still get the care they need. Senior doctors and other doctors not on strike will be working. Patients should go to their appointments unless told not to. The strike is about pay and job chances for these doctors. It might be hard for the NHS, but they have plans to keep services safe. If you are sick, call 111 or 999 for emergencies, or see your regular doctor for less urgent problems. The government and doctors are trying to fix the problem.

articles.title: Artemis Astronauts Lose Contact with Earth
articles.summary: The Artemis astronauts are flying far from Earth to the Moon. They usually talk to mission control in Houston, Texas, using radio and laser signals. But on Monday, they will pass behind the Moon, and the Moon will block these signals. For about 40 minutes, they will have no contact with Earth. This means they will be alone in space with their thoughts. Victor Glover, one of the astronauts, hopes people on Earth will send good thoughts during this time. Long ago, Apollo astronauts like Michael Collins also lost contact when they went behind the Moon. He felt alone but not scared. On Earth, places like Goonhilly in England use big antennas to track the spacecraft and help NASA.

articles.title: More Money for Families with Kids
articles.summary: In the UK, the government is giving more money to families who receive benefits. A rule that stopped parents from getting extra money for more than two children has ended. This means about 480,000 families with three or more kids will get about £4,100 more each year. A mom named Tracey, who has five children, says this extra money helps her pay for food and other things because prices are going up. She works full-time but still struggles. The change happens automatically, so parents don't need to apply. Other benefits, like for people with disabilities, are also increasing by 3.8% to match higher prices. Some people think the money could be used better elsewhere, but many families are happy.

articles.title: UCLA Wins First Women's Basketball Championship
articles.summary: UCLA's women's basketball team won their first NCAA national championship by beating South Carolina 79-51. The game was played in Phoenix, and UCLA players like Gabriela Jaquez and Lauren Betts scored many points to help their team win. The team had set this goal after losing in the Final Four last year, and they worked hard all season with only one loss. Coach Cori Close was very happy and proud of the players for their teamwork and character. This is UCLA's first title since 1978, and the players celebrated with confetti and fans.

articles.title: A Brave Rescue in Iran
articles.summary: A US airman was rescued in a dangerous mission in Iran after his fighter jet was shot down. The jet had two people on board, and the pilot was saved quickly, but the airman was missing. Iran wanted to capture him and offered money for his capture. The airman hid in a mountain crack and used a beacon to help rescuers find him. Many special forces, warplanes, helicopters, and the CIA worked together to save him. President Trump announced the rescue on social media. This was the first time in over 20 years that a US fighter jet was shot down by enemy fire. The airman only had a handgun to protect himself while waiting for help.

articles.title: Your Body's Natural Cleanup Crew
articles.summary: This article talks about how our bodies can clean themselves without needing special products. Many companies sell things like detox teas, foot pads, diets, juice cleanses, and colon flushes that promise to remove toxins and make you healthier. But experts say most people don't need these products because our bodies already have a strong natural system to get rid of unwanted chemicals. For example, our liver, kidneys, and colon work together to filter out bad stuff. Instead of buying expensive detox items, we can help our bodies by eating more fruits and vegetables, drinking water, and following healthy habits. Scientists haven't found good evidence that detox products actually improve health for healthy adults. So, it's better to trust our body's own superpowers and save money by not buying unnecessary cleanses.
